skill.name: azure-ai
skill.description: Guides Azure AI tasks for Search, Speech, OpenAI, Document Intelligence, Vision, Translation, and Content Safety. It points users to Azure MCP commands and condensed SDK references for common implementation patterns.
skill.summary: Guide Azure AI service selection, MCP usage, and SDK implementation patterns.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, environment, network, and heuristic signals, but review shows they are documentation examples and Azure service references. No executable scripts, prompt injection attempts, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: Documentation Commands Misclassified as Code Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged command patterns are markdown install commands, Azure CLI names, and MCP command names. The skill does not execute them automatically. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: I inspected the main skill file and SDK references. The evidence is documentation text and fenced examples, not runnable skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation snippets and command names. No file contains executable wrapper code that invokes these commands.

low_findings.title: Credential Environment Variables Used in SDK Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE with minor caution. The examples read Azure endpoint and key variables to construct SDK clients. They do not print, transmit, or collect secrets outside normal service authentication. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: Environment access is limited to quick-start snippets. Users should still avoid pasting real secrets into prompts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples access named Azure credentials for normal SDK initialization. I found no evidence of logging, exfiltration, or broad environment scraping.

low_findings.title: Microsoft Learn Links Misclassified as Network Risk
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs point to Microsoft Learn documentation for Azure AI Search and Speech. They are reference links, not network calls. Confidence: 0.98. Confidence reasoning: The lines are markdown links in a documentation section. No code path fetches these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is static markdown links to official documentation. There is no runtime network request or external callback.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The analyzer combined documentation commands, SDK credential examples, and documentation links into a dangerous pattern. Manual review found no malicious intent, prompt injection, obfuscation, or data exfiltration. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: All scanned files are markdown guidance. The suspicious combination disappears when the snippets are interpreted as reference material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The critical heuristic depends on aggregating unrelated documentation examples. I found no executable code or instruction to collect and send credentials.

content.user_title: Build Azure AI Search and Speech Workflows
content.value_statement: Azure AI work spans many services, SDKs, and command paths. This skill gives focused routing, MCP commands, and concise SDK references for common Azure AI tasks.
actual_capabilities: Routes tasks across Azure AI Search, Speech, OpenAI, Document Intelligence, Vision, Translation, and Content Safety.
Lists Azure MCP commands for search index listing, search index lookup, search queries, transcription, and speech synthesis.
Summarizes SDK setup patterns for Python, TypeScript, .NET, and Java Azure AI clients.
Highlights search patterns including full-text search, vector search, hybrid search, semantic search, and AI enrichment.
Provides quick references for OCR, transcription, translation, content moderation, and image analysis workflows.
Links users to Microsoft Learn documentation for deeper Azure AI Search and Speech guidance.
limitations: It does not cover Azure Functions, databases, or general Azure resource management.
Direct MCP actions require Azure MCP to be enabled in the user environment.
The SDK references are condensed and do not replace full product documentation.
It does not provision Azure resources or manage credentials by itself.

use_cases.title: Plan Search Relevance Work
use_cases.description: Compare full-text, vector, hybrid, and semantic search options before choosing an Azure AI Search implementation path.

use_cases.title: Implement Speech Features
use_cases.description: Find the right Azure Speech command or SDK pattern for transcription, synthesis, diarization, and streaming scenarios.

use_cases.title: Select Azure AI Services
use_cases.description: Map product needs to Azure AI Search, OpenAI, Vision, Translation, Content Safety, or Document Intelligence.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Right Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the correct Azure AI service for this task: [describe task]. Explain what to use and what to avoid.

prompt_templates.title: Query a Search Index
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Azure AI Search guidance to help me list indexes and query the index named [index name].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Speech Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Azure Speech workflow for [batch or real-time] transcription with [language], timestamps, and speaker handling.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a RAG Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a retrieval augmented generation architecture using Azure AI Search and Azure OpenAI for [domain]. Include indexing, retrieval, ranking, and security considerations.

output_examples.input: I need semantic product search over a catalog with embeddings and filters.
output_examples.output: Use Azure AI Search with hybrid search, vector fields, semantic ranking, and filters. Define vector dimensions to match the embedding model.

output_examples.input: I need to turn meeting audio into searchable notes.
output_examples.output: Use Azure Speech transcription with diarization and timestamps. Store transcript segments in Azure AI Search for later querying.

output_examples.input: I need to extract fields from invoices and review low-confidence values.
output_examples.output: Use Document Intelligence with a prebuilt invoice model first. Add confidence thresholds and route uncertain fields for review.

best_practices: Use Azure MCP commands when available, then fall back to SDK references for implementation details.
Prefer Entra ID or managed credentials for production workloads where the Azure SDK supports them.
Choose only the Azure AI features needed for each request to reduce cost, latency, and operational complexity.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for Azure Functions, databases, or broad Azure administration tasks.
Do not paste production secrets into prompts while adapting the credential examples.
Do not enable every AI feature by default when a narrower service call would meet the requirement.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Azure commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents Azure MCP commands and SDK patterns. Actual execution depends on the tools enabled in the user environment.

faq.question: Which Azure AI services does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Azure AI Search, Speech, OpenAI, Document Intelligence, Vision, Translation, and Content Safety.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it replace Microsoft Learn documentation?
faq.answer: No. It provides concise guidance and links to deeper Microsoft Learn pages for detailed product documentation.

faq.question: Does it manage Azure credentials?
faq.answer: No. It shows SDK credential patterns, but users must manage secrets and identity configuration securely.

faq.question: When should I avoid this skill?
faq.answer: Avoid it for Azure Functions, database services, and general Azure resource management tasks.
